Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Saint Francis Memorial Hospital To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-05-2013 Continued To Mar-14-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci's Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Saint Francis Memorial Hospital To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions is continued to March 14, 2013 for further briefing as set forth herein. The Court previously ordered the parties to file joint separate statements. However, the parties complied with the order by filing joint separate statements which are 929 pages long for the production requests propounded on Dignity Health and 267 pages long for the production requests propounded on Saint Francis Memorial Hospital. The purpose of a separate statement is for each party to state its arguments succinctly and convincingly. (See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial , 8:1151.1.) Here, the responses in the joint separate statements are repetitive, derivative and unwieldy, making it impractical for the court to rule on the matters in dispute. In order to avoid the cost of reference, the parties are ordered to file and serve a brief in support of and a brief in opposition to the requests for production still at each. Each brief shall be no longer than 15 pages. The discovery disputes shall be grouped by category, and the heading of each section shall state the general subject matter and, immediately thereafter, the numbers of each request for production falling within that category. (See Weil & Brown, supra, at 8:1154 - 8:1154.1.) Plaintiff shall file and serve by email his brief in support of the motion by no later than February 20, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Defendants shall file and serve by email any brief in opposition no later than March 4, 2013 at 4:00 pm. No reply shall be filed. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, on the date of filing. The parties are encouraged to continue their meet and confer efforts to resolve their discovery disputes informally. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
